free_text: Mass, charge and energy distributions in the very
asymmetric fission of 249Cf induced by thermal
neutrons
short description;
part of the tabulated data
- short description;
prelim.data
R.H.,H.O.D.
H.R.F.
B.D.W.,J.C.G.
The ILL high-flux reactor
Thermal neutrons.
The Lohengrin spectrometer was used. It is
dispersive in two dimensions. The energy of the
fragments and the mass-to-charge ratio were
determined from measuring the horizontal and vertical
coordinates in a two-dimensional ionization chamber.
A delta-E signal was also obtained from it.
25 ug of Cf2O3, enriched to 99.73% in Cf-249,
with an area of 2.5 x 20 mm2, electrodeposited
onto a titanium backing. The target was covered
with a 0.25 um nickel foil.
Corrections for changes in the target properties
as a function of exposure time were applied.
Gaussian-peak unfolding of the spectra.
Since Djebara et al. (see REL-REF) obtained their
yields by normalizing relative count rates (A = 88
to 120) to 100%, the addition of the present yields,
summing to about 1.5% plus a contribution from the
symmetric region of 0.7% would give 102.2% for the
total light peak yield. In consequence, the obtained
yields were divided by 1.022.
The same correction factor (see ANALYSIS) should be
applied to the chain yields published by Djebara et al
No direct information from main reference.
It seems likely that errors include both statistical
and fitting errors.
J.B.
Date is corrected. BIB information added.
Upper case to low case converted
SD: Refs added. Cosmetic corrections.
COMMENT deleted. Info moved to MONITOR(-REF).
SD: Corrections in Subents 002,004-006.
All yields are integrated over the kinetic
energy distribution. The yields are
normalized to 100% for each mass.
Table 1 of Nucl.Phys.,A571(1994)427.
Units changed to PC/FIS. SF8=REL deleted.

PC/FIS -> NO-DIM. Titles ELEMENT and MASS were swapped.
COMMENT -> ANALYSIS.
Zp is coded as most probable charge,
but what is quoted in the main reference
is mean nuclear charge. However, it is likely
that this is actually also most probable
charge, because the distributions are
to first order gaussian.
The standard deviation sigma-Z used
when determining the mean nuclear charge.

The main reference gives most probable
energy. However, the numbers have been obtained
by performing Gaussian fits to the distributions,
and therefore it is actually also the average
kinetic energy.
Width of the energy distribution.

Fractional independent yields FIY(A,Z,E) (%) at
various kinetic energies E(MeV) of the fission
fragments.
Low bin-limit for the kinetic energy
of the fission fragments. The values are integrated
over the ionic charge distribution. The energies
are corrected for energy loss in the target.
High bin-limit for the kinetic energy
of the fission fragments. The values are integrated
over the ionic charge distribution. The energies
are corrected for energy loss in the target.
The energies are corrected for energy loss in the
target.
Data have been normalized to 100% for each
energy bin and mass.
